candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows dir=SHORT with conv=100% but green_matches=0, the live signal has no historical analog and must be sized down, not up — high live conviction without a memory match is a regime-novelty trap.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: green_matches counts how many times the current indicator configuration produced a profitable outcome in historical memory. AUDUSD shows 14/30 bear, conv=100%, hist_hr=70% — but green_matches=0 means the EXACT live pattern has never been seen winning. Institutional flow that produces a 'never-before-seen' consensus config is usually a one-sided liquidation cascade that mean-reverts violently once stops are swept, not a durable trend. Conviction measures agreement among indicators; green_matches measures whether that agreement has ever paid. They are orthogonal.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conv >= 0.90 and green_matches == 0: size *= 0.5  # high conviction with zero memory match = novelty trap, halve size
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces drawdown contribution from false-breakout entries; expected Sharpe +0.02-0.03 by avoiding the largest single-trade losers that come from chasing unmatched 100%-conviction signals.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: AUDUSD live: 2/30 bull, 14/30 bear, dir=SHORT, conv=100%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=70%. EURUSD similarly: conv=58%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=66%. Every pair currently shows green_matches=0 despite non-zero hist_hr — the live configs are not matching historical winners, so the hist_hr is from DIFFERENT configurations and cannot be trusted for these live entries.
